name: incident_response
description: a security incident-response lifecycle world; actions detect, triage, contain, eradicate, recover

card.description: a security incident-response lifecycle world; actions detect, triage, contain, eradicate, recover

rules: The lifecycle is strictly ordered: monitoring -> detected -> triaged -> contained -> eradicated -> recovered. Each action advances exactly one phase and only from the immediately preceding phase.
'detect' fires only while monitoring and only if a threat is present; it marks the incident detected.
'triage' (from detected) confirms scope and scores severity and risk from the number of affected hosts and whether data was exfiltrated.
'contain' (from triaged) isolates affected systems: it stops the spread and cuts risk, but takes services offline.
'eradicate' (from contained) removes the threat from the environment.
'recover' (from eradicated) restores services and drives residual risk to zero, resolving the incident.
Any action taken out of order is a no-op and leaves the state unchanged.

transition.code:
def transition(state, action):
    s = dict(state)
    name = action.get("name")
    phase = s.get("phase", "monitoring")

    def advance(to_phase):
        # Only genuine phase transitions touch state, so out-of-order actions
        # stay exact no-ops and the lifecycle graph remains a clean chain.
        log = list(s.get("timeline", []))
        log.append(name)
        s["timeline"] = log
        s["phase"] = to_phase

    if name == "detect":
        # Surface a live intrusion the monitoring stack has not yet flagged.
        if phase == "monitoring" and s.get("threat_present"):
            s["detected"] = True
            advance("detected")
    elif name == "triage":
        # Confirm scope and score severity/risk from the blast radius.
        if phase == "detected":
            hosts = int(s.get("affected_hosts", 0))
            leaked = 2 if int(s.get("data_exfiltrated", 0)) > 0 else 0
            sev = min(10, hosts * 2 + leaked)
            s["severity"] = sev
            s["risk"] = sev
            s["scope_known"] = True
            advance("triaged")
    elif name == "contain":
        # Isolate affected systems: spread stops, but service goes offline.
        if phase == "triaged":
            s["contained"] = True
            s["services_online"] = False
            s["risk"] = max(0, int(s.get("risk", 0)) - 4)
            advance("contained")
    elif name == "eradicate":
        # Remove the adversary's foothold from the environment.
        if phase == "contained":
            s["threat_present"] = False
            s["eradicated"] = True
            s["risk"] = max(0, int(s.get("risk", 0)) - 2)
            advance("eradicated")
    elif name == "recover":
        # Restore clean systems to production; the incident is resolved.
        if phase == "eradicated":
            s["recovered"] = True
            s["services_online"] = True
            s["risk"] = 0
            advance("recovered")
    return s

emit.report: incident [{phase}] severity {severity}/10, risk {risk}, {affected_hosts} hosts affected, {data_exfiltrated} records lost; recovered={recovered}, services_online={services_online}
